title: calceolaria corymbosa subsp. santiagina
intro: Calceolaria corymbosa subsp. santiagina, also known as the Santiagian slipper flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Calceolaria corymbosa subsp. santiagina have been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, fever reduction, and the alleviation of respiratory issues. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its leaves in hot water, or by using it in a topical ointment or salve to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
